engineGuide.h2: Nissan Leopard Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Leopard across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The RB20DET powered the F31 Leopard's performance variants, sharing architecture with the Skyline R31 - a turbocharged icon among JDM enthusiasts.
compatibleVariants: F31 Base Turbo, F31 Ultima, F31 J. Ferraz Edition
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal failure leading to smoke (60k-100k miles)
Timing belt stretch on interference engine
AFM sensor degradation causing rich running
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB20DET Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated RB20E served as the entry-level F31 engine, offering reliable SOHC performance without turbo complexity.
commonFailures: Coil pack failure causing misfire at high mileage
Distributor cap wear on early models
Oil pump wear leading to low pressure
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB20E Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The VG30E was the foundational V6 for the Leopard F31 and Infiniti M30, offering smooth SOHC power but requiring strict timing belt maintenance.
compatibleVariants: F31 Base 3.0, Infiniti M30, F31 Standard V6
commonFailures: Timing belt snap causing catastrophic valve damage (interference)
Valve cover gasket oil leaks (common at 80k+ miles)
Rear main seal failure leading to oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for VG30E Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The DOHC VG30DE delivered higher output for performance-oriented F31 models, but added complexity to timing belt service intervals.
compatibleVariants: F31 VG30DE Trim, F31 Sport V6, F31 High Output
commonFailures: Valve cover gasket leaks at cylinder head joints
Hydraulic lifter tick on high-mileage units
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for VG30DE Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The rare VG30DET brought twin-turbo V6 power to the F31 Leopard, creating a highly sought-after collector engine with 255hp output.
compatibleVariants: F31 VG30DET Turbo, F31 Limited Turbo Edition
commonFailures: Turbo bearing failure due to oil starvation
Intercooler piping degradation causing boost loss
Timing belt failure risk on interference design
entries.cta: Get quotes for VG30DET Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The compact VG20DET delivered turbocharged V6 performance in a 2.0L package, a rare JDM-only configuration for the F31 Leopard.
compatibleVariants: F31 VG20DET Trim, F31 Compact Turbo
commonFailures: Small turbo overheating under sustained load
V6 timing belt complexity increasing service cost
Oil cooler line leaks at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for VG20DET Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The VG20E provided smooth SOHC V6 character for base Y33 Leopard models, prioritising refinement over outright performance.
compatibleVariants: Y33 Base 2.0, Y33 Standard Trim, JY33 Entry Level
commonFailures: Timing belt wear requiring preventative replacement
Oil pump drive wear on high-mileage examples
Intake manifold gasket leaks causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for VG20E Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: The VQ25DE introduced refined 2.5L V6 power to the Y33 Leopard, featuring Nissan's acclaimed VQ architecture with improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: Y33 VQ25, Y33 Standard 2.5, JY33 Mid-Spec
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start
Oil consumption from piston ring wear (100k+ miles)
VVT solenoid clogging causing timing errors
entries.cta: Get quotes for VQ25DE Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The VQ30DE became the flagship Y33 Leopard engine, delivering smooth 3.0L V6 power with the reliability Nissan's VQ series is known for.
compatibleVariants: Y33 VQ30, Y33 High Spec, Y33 Ultima, JY33 Premium
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear causing rattle (80k-120k miles)
Valve cover gasket leaks at camshaft seals
Water pump bearing failure leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for VQ30DE Nissan Leopard engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The VQ30DD featured direct injection technology for the Y33 Leopard, offering improved efficiency but requiring specialist servicing knowledge.
compatibleVariants: Y33 VQ30DD, Y33 Direct Injection Trim, JY33 DI Spec
commonFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting
Timing chain tensioner failure on early production
entries.cta: Get quotes for VQ30DD Nissan Leopard engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Leopard Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Leopard's RB, VG and VQ engine families each have distinct reliability profiles. Timing belt failures on VG30 interference engines and turbo oil starvation on RB20DET units dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: VG30E/VG30DE Timing Belt Failure - Why Leopard V6 Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Leopard F31 VG30E, VG30DE (1986-1992, VG30E & VG30DE)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced preventatively
problems.rootCause: Interference engine design means snapped timing belt causes valves to strike pistons, destroying cylinder head and often the block.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing belt, tensioner, water pump replacement before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic damage; recommended every 60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve replacement, head resurfacing, gasket set after belt snap
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block undamaged; labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt VG30 engine with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On a Nissan Leopard worth £3,000-£8,000, preventative timing belt replacement is essential. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine from our network offers better value than dealer cylinder head repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt VG30 engine prices for your Nissan Leopard

problems.h4: RB20DET Turbo Failure - Why Leopard Turbo Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation or clogged feed lines cause turbo bearing wear, leading to shaft play, oil burning, and eventual turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo removal, bearing replacement, seal renewal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine oil system uncontaminated

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full oil system clean, new turbo, filter replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses metal contamination risk from failed turbo

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt RB20DET with upgraded turbo components
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when metal debris has damaged bearings

problems.recommendation: If your RB20DET is smoking heavily or has lost boost, get an oil sample test first. If metal particles are present, replacement is safer than repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt RB20DET Nissan Leopard engines

problems.h4: VG30DE Oil Leaks - Why Leopard V6 Engines Consume Oil
problems.rootCause: Age-hardened valve cover gaskets and rear main seal allow oil to escape, leading to low oil levels and potential bearing damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve cover gaskets, cam seals, rear main seal replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if caught early before bearing wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Gaskets plus valve stem seals, PCV service
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses oil consumption from multiple sources

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded seal materials
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil starvation has damaged bearings

problems.recommendation: Small oil leaks are common on high-mileage VG30DE units. If consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles, investigate bearing clearance before committing to gasket repair.
problems.cta: Compare VG30DE engine replacement prices for your Leopard

problems.h4: VQ30DE Timing Chain Rattle - Why Y33 Leopard Engines Make Noise
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic chain tensioners lose pressure over time, allowing chain slap against guides, which can break and cause timing disruption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioners and chain guides
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before guide fragmentation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain, guides, tensioners, sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive fix for rattle and wear prevention

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if chain jump has caused valve contact

problems.recommendation: Cold-start rattle on a Y33 Leopard warrants immediate inspection. If guides are intact, tensioner replacement is cost-effective; if not, budget for replacement.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt VQ30DE engines for your Nissan Leopard Y33

problems.h4: RB20DET Misfire & Ignition Failure - Why Leopard Turbo Engines Run Rough
problems.rootCause: Ageing coil packs, cracked distributor caps, or failing CAS sensors cause intermittent misfire, reducing power and increasing fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Coil packs, cap, rotor, spark plugs replacement
repairOptions.longevity: First-line fix for misfire; often resolves issue

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: CAS sensor, AFM, wiring harness inspection and repair
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses electrical causes of intermittent misfire

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine if misfire caused mechanical damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only if prolonged misfire led to piston/cylinder damage

problems.recommendation: Start with ignition components and sensor diagnostics. Only consider engine replacement if compression testing reveals mechanical damage from prolonged misfire.
problems.cta: Get quotes for RB20DET ignition repair or replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Leopard
finalCta.paragraph: The Nissan Leopard's enthusiast appeal means owners invest in keeping these rare JDM engines running. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand RB, VG and VQ platform nuances. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Leopard Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Leopard and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Leopard Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Leopard is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure points to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan Leopard 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L petrol engines powered the entry-level and performance F31 Leopard variants, featuring Nissan's RB and VG families with both naturally aspirated and turbocharged configurations for different driving priorities.
commonFailurePoints: RB20DET turbo oil starvation; VG20 timing belt wear on interference design
items.cta: Compare Nissan Leopard 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Leopard 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L VQ25DE brought refined V6 character to the later Y33 Leopard generation, offering improved fuel efficiency and smoother power delivery than the earlier VG-series engines it replaced.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner rattle; oil consumption from piston ring wear at high mileage
items.cta: Compare Nissan Leopard 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Leopard 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L displacement dominated the Leopard range across both generations, with VG30E/VG30DE/VG30DET powering the F31 and VQ30DE/VQ30DD serving the Y33, offering outputs from 160hp to 255hp depending on configuration. Not sure which engine size your Nissan Leopard needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Nissan Leopard specialists.

engineYears.h2: Nissan Leopard Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Leopard's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Leopard launched with the VG30E 3.0L SOHC V6, establishing the foundation for the F31 generation's engine lineup.
keyChanges: F31 chassis introduced with rear-wheel drive layout
Platform sharing with Skyline R31 and Laurel C32 confirmed
knownFor: Smooth V6 character but requires strict timing belt maintenance
Interference engine design means belt failure is catastrophic
Strong donor compatibility with Skyline R31 for parts
years.cta: Get quotes for 1986 Nissan Leopard replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Leopard VG30E timing belt • F31 3.0 engine replacement • Infiniti M30 engine UK

years.description: Performance variants arrived with the turbocharged RB20DET and high-output VG30DE, defining the F31's enthusiast appeal.
keyChanges: RB20DET 2.0L turbo introduced for performance trims
VG30DE DOHC 3.0L added for higher-output V6 models
Ultima and J. Ferraz special editions launched
knownFor: RB20DET becomes most-searched Leopard engine due to Skyline sharing
VG30DE adds complexity but delivers 230hp in top spec
Turbo models require diligent oil maintenance to avoid bearing failure
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1988 Nissan Leopard replacement engines
years.ticker: RB20DET turbo failure • VG30DE oil leak repair • F31 performance engine replacement

years.description: Rare factory turbo V6 engines appeared, creating highly collectible Leopard variants with 200-255hp outputs.
keyChanges: VG20DET 2.0L turbo V6 added for compact performance
Final F31 production years see trim rationalisation

VG20DET - 2.0L turbo V6 petrol (Compact turbo F31)

Small turbo VG20DET prone to heat-related failure
All turbo F31 engines require specialist JDM knowledge for servicing

keyChanges: Y33 chassis replaces F31; front-wheel drive optional on some markets
knownFor: VQ engines chain-driven, eliminating belt failure risk
Timing chain tensioners can rattle at high mileage
Y33 mounting points differ from F31 - engines not interchangeable

faq.h2: Nissan Leopard Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Will a Nissan Skyline R31 RB20DET fit my Leopard F31?
items.answer: Yes, the RB20DET from Skyline R31 is a direct fit for F31 Leopard as both share the same platform and engine mounting points. This makes Skyline donors a cost-effective source for Leopard RB20DET replacements.
keyPoints: F31 Leopard and Skyline R31 share chassis architecture
RB20DET mounting points, ECU and wiring are compatible
Always verify engine code matches your Leopard's original spec
Laurel C32 and Cefiro A31 also share this platform
items.cta: Compare Skyline R31 RB20DET donor prices for your Leopard

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Nissan Leopard engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Nissan Leopard engines typically range from £1,200 for base VG30E units to £5,500 for rare turbo variants like the VG30DET, with supply-only pricing; fitted costs add £800-£2,200 depending on workshop.
keyPoints: RB20DET rebuilt: £1,800-£4,500 supply only
VG30 series rebuilt: £1,200-£3,400 supply only
VQ series rebuilt: £1,500-£4,200 supply only
Supply & fit adds £800-£2,200 labour depending on complexity
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for your Nissan Leopard engine replacement

items.question: What is the difference between VG30E and VG30DE engines?
items.answer: VG30E is a SOHC 3.0L V6 producing 160-180hp, while VG30DE is a DOHC version delivering 185-230hp; both are interference engines requiring strict timing belt maintenance to avoid catastrophic failure.
keyPoints: VG30E: Single overhead cam, simpler, lower output
VG30DE: Dual overhead cam, higher revving, more power
Both use same block but different cylinder heads and valvetrain
Timing belt replacement interval: every 60,000 miles for both
items.cta: Compare VG30E vs VG30DE engine prices for your Leopard

items.question: Is the Nissan RB20DET an interference engine?
items.answer: Yes, the RB20DET is an interference engine, meaning a snapped timing belt will cause valves to strike pistons, potentially destroying the cylinder head and requiring full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Interference design demands preventative belt replacement
Recommended belt change interval: 60,000 miles or 5 years
Always replace tensioner and water pump during belt service
Keep records of belt changes for resale and warranty purposes
items.cta: Get quotes for RB20DET timing belt service or engine replacement

items.question: What is the engine code for my Nissan Leopard?
items.answer: Your Leopard's engine code is stamped on the block near the cylinder head; common codes include RB20DET (2.0 turbo), VG30E (3.0 SOHC), VG30DE (3.0 DOHC), and VQ30DE (Y33 3.0 V6).
keyPoints: Check engine bay sticker or VIN decoder for code confirmation
F31 models use RB or VG codes; Y33 models use VQ codes
Engine code determines compatible replacement options
Provide code when requesting quotes to ensure correct fitment
items.cta: Identify your Nissan Leopard engine code and get matching quotes

items.question: Can I fit an RB25DET into a Leopard F31 as an upgrade?
items.answer: Yes, the RB25DET from R33/R34 Skyline can be swapped into an F31 Leopard with custom mounts and ECU adaptation, offering a popular performance upgrade over the stock RB20DET.
keyPoints: RB25DET shares architecture with RB20DET but has 2.5L displacement
Requires custom engine mounts and wiring harness adaptation
ECU remap essential for reliable operation post-swap
Budget £3,000-£6,000 for professional conversion including labour
items.cta: Enquire about RB25DET swap packages for your Nissan Leopard F31

items.question: Are Nissan Leopard and Infiniti M30 engines interchangeable?
items.answer: Yes, the Infiniti M30's VG30E engine is identical to the F31 Leopard VG30E, making M30 donors a viable and often more available source for Leopard engine replacements in the UK.
keyPoints: M30 VG30E shares block, heads and ancillaries with Leopard VG30E
ECU and wiring may require minor adaptation for Leopard chassis
M30 engines often more readily available in UK breaker yards
Always verify compression and service history before purchase
items.cta: Compare Infiniti M30 VG30E engine prices for Leopard replacement

items.question: What causes VG30DE oil leaks on Nissan Leopard?
items.answer: VG30DE oil leaks typically stem from age-hardened valve cover gaskets, camshaft seals or the rear main seal; while not immediately catastrophic, chronic leaks can lead to low oil levels and bearing damage.
keyPoints: Valve cover gaskets harden and crack after 80k+ miles
Rear main seal replacement requires gearbox removal
Monitor oil level weekly if leaks are present
Address leaks early to avoid costly bearing damage from oil starvation
items.cta: Get quotes for VG30DE gasket repair or engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with reconditioned Nissan Leopard engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Nissan Leopard engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for defects in materials or workmanship.
keyPoints: Warranty valid at any UK specialist within our network
Covers internal components, seals and gaskets against failure
Does not cover damage from improper installation or maintenance
Always retain invoice and warranty documentation for claims
items.cta: View warranty terms and compare reconditioned Leopard engine suppliers

items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who source and supply Nissan Leopard engines, including JDM import experts familiar with RB, VG and VQ families for F31 and Y33 models.
keyPoints: Specialists understand JDM engine import regulations and documentation
Network includes fitters experienced with Leopard chassis specifics
Compare multiple quotes to find best value for your engine code
All suppliers provide minimum 12-month warranty on rebuilt units
items.cta: Compare UK Nissan Leopard engine suppliers and get quotes today
